refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall enable monitoring, controlling, and optimizing energy consumption to meet the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad; decomposing it into monitoring, control, and optimization clarifies how energy efficiency can be achieved systematically.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by measuring energy_per_ton over the evaluation period and confirming it meets ≤ E_target; validate monitoring data accuracy, control actions, and optimization effectiveness.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Deploy energy and load sensors with telemetry data aggregation.
DP2: Implement adaptive vehicle control algorithms to reduce energy consumption.
DP3: Use predictive analytics and scheduling optimization for task planning.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address monitoring, control, and optimization respectively.

potential_sources: Data dependency between monitoring (DP1) and control (DP2)
Feedback loop from optimization (DP3) to control (DP2)
coupling_check.notes: Coupling is expected and manageable; clear interfaces and modular design mitigate risks.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable sensor data and network connectivity.
Risk of control algorithm complexity impacting real-time responsiveness.
Optimization effectiveness depends on quality of input data and models.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Ensure modular separation of monitoring, control, and optimization to reduce complexity and facilitate verification.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level integration of energy monitoring, control, and optimization functions.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Used for real-time energy and operational data exchange between Vehicle and NGHE Solution.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational tasks and schedules from higher-level planning modules.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Interfaces with Safety Services to ensure energy efficiency actions comply with safety policies.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Communication backbone supporting data exchange among Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services, and NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes energy efficiency logic and data aggregation; low diffusion risk.
Contains sensors and control actuators; moderate diffusion due to embedded control.
Limited role in energy efficiency; mostly remote control, higher diffusion risk if logic duplicated.
Primarily safety-focused; minimal containment for energy efficiency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Energy efficiency functions are best contained within the NGHE Solution module to centralize logic and minimize diffusion.
containment_risks: Potential performance bottlenecks if all processing centralized.
Dependency on reliable network links for telemetry data.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Consider partitioning control functions partially into Vehicle module for real-time responsiveness while keeping optimization centralized.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DPs logically satisfy the refined FRs with clear traceability and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Risk of duplicated control logic if Vehicle module also implements control algorithms.
Network dependency may cause delays in telemetry data affecting control.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores favor NGHE Solution as primary owner; Vehicle module involvement should be carefully scoped.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via telemetry data analysis and operational testing of control and optimization modules.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of functions to NGHE Solution with defined interfaces to Vehicle and Safety Services.

recommended_revisions: Refine interface definitions to ensure real-time data flow requirements are met.
Evaluate partitioning of control algorithms between NGHE Solution and Vehicle for latency-sensitive operations.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition and assignments meet the requirements and constraints with acceptable risks and clear traceability.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor energy consumption and tonnage moved in real-time.
FR2: Control vehicle operations to minimize energy usage per ton moved.
FR3: Optimize operational parameters and task scheduling to improve energy efficiency.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Sufficient computational resources in NGHE Solution module.
open_risks: Potential latency in telemetry affecting control responsiveness.
Complexity of optimization algorithms impacting real-time operation.
evidence_needed: Performance benchmarks of control algorithms in Vehicle and NGHE Solution.
Validation of telemetry data accuracy and timeliness.
